7_8_2.MSG: But Gideon replied, "What have I done compared to you? Why, even the gleanings of Ephraim are superior to the vintage of Abiezer.
7_8_2.NIV: But he answered them, “What have I accomplished compared to you? Aren’t the gleanings of Ephraim’s grapes better than the full grape harvest of Abiezer?
7_8_2.NRSVCE: So he said to them, “What have I done now in comparison with you? Is not the gleaning of the grapes of Ephraim better than the vintage of Abiezer?

7_8_3.NKJV: God has delivered into your hands the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb. And what was I able to do in comparison with you?” Then their anger toward him subsided when he said that.
7_8_3.KJV: God hath delivered into your hands the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb: and what was I able to do in comparison of you? Then their anger was abated toward him, when he had said that. anger: Heb. spirit
7_8_3.ESV: God has given into your hands the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb. What have I been able to do in comparison with you?” Then their anger against him subsided when he said this.
7_8_3.NASB: 'God has given the leaders of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb into your hands; and what was I able to do in comparison with you?' Then their anger toward him subsided when he said that.
7_8_3.NLT: God gave you victory over Oreb and Zeeb, the commanders of the Midianite army. What have I accomplished compared to that?” When the men of Ephraim heard Gideon’s answer, their anger subsided.
7_8_3.WEB: God has delivered into your hand the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb! What was I able to do in comparison with you?” Then their anger was abated toward him, when he had said that.
7_8_3.CSB17: God handed over to you Oreb and Zeeb, the two princes of Midian. What was I able to do compared to you?” When he said this, their anger against him subsided.
7_8_3.AMP: God has given the leaders of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb into your hands; and what was I able to do in comparison with you?" Then their anger toward him subsided when he made this statement.
7_8_3.MSG: God gave you Midian's commanders, Oreb and Zeeb. What have I done compared with you?"When they heard this, they calmed down and cooled off.
7_8_3.NIV: God gave Oreb and Zeeb, the Midianite leaders, into your hands. What was I able to do compared to you?” At this, their resentment against him subsided.
7_8_3.NRSVCE: God has given into your hands the captains of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; what have I been able to do in comparison with you?” When he said this, their anger against him subsided.

7_8_4.NKJV: When Gideon came to the Jordan, he and the three hundred men who were with him crossed over, exhausted but still in pursuit.
7_8_4.KJV: And Gideon came to Jordan, and passed over, he, and the three hundred men that were with him, faint, yet pursuing them.
7_8_4.ESV: And Gideon came to the Jordan and crossed over, he and the 300 men who were with him, exhausted yet pursuing.
7_8_4.NASB: Then Gideon and the 300 men who were with him came to the Jordan [and] crossed over, weary yet pursuing.
7_8_4.NLT: Gideon then crossed the Jordan River with his 300 men, and though exhausted, they continued to chase the enemy.
7_8_4.WEB: Gideon came to the Jordan, and passed over, he, and the three hundred men who were with him, faint, yet pursuing.
7_8_4.CSB17: Gideon and the three hundred men came to the Jordan and crossed it. They were exhausted but still in pursuit.
7_8_4.AMP: So Gideon came to the Jordan and crossed over [the river], he and the three hundred men who were with him—exhausted, yet [still] pursuing [the enemy].
7_8_4.MSG: Gideon and his three hundred arrived at the Jordan and crossed over. They were bone-tired but still pressing the pursuit.
7_8_4.NIV: Gideon and his three hundred men, exhausted yet keeping up the pursuit, came to the Jordan and crossed it.
7_8_4.NRSVCE: Then Gideon came to the Jordan and crossed over, he and the three hundred who were with him, exhausted and famished.

7_8_5.NKJV: Then he said to the men of Succoth, “Please give loaves of bread to the people who follow me, for they are exhausted, and I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.KJV: And he said unto the men of Succoth, Give, I pray you, loaves of bread unto the people that follow me; for they be faint, and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian.
7_8_5.ESV: So he said to the men of Succoth, “Please give loaves of bread to the people who follow me, for they are exhausted, and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.NASB: He said to the men of Succoth, 'Please give loaves of bread to the people who are following me, for they are weary, and I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.'
7_8_5.NLT: When they reached Succoth, Gideon asked the leaders of the town, “Please give my warriors some food. They are very tired. I am chasing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.WEB: He said to the men of Succoth, “Please give loaves of bread to the people who follow me; for they are faint, and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.CSB17: He said to the men of Succoth, “Please give some loaves of bread to the troops under my command, because they are exhausted, for I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.AMP: He said to the men of Succoth, "Please give loaves of bread to the people who are following me since they are exhausted, and I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian."
7_8_5.MSG: He asked the men of Succoth, "Please, give me some loaves of bread for my troops I have with me. They're worn out, and I'm hot on the trail of Zebah and Zalmunna, the Midianite kings."
7_8_5.NIV: He said to the men of Succoth, “Give my troops some bread; they are worn out, and I am still pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”
7_8_5.NRSVCE: So he said to the people of Succoth, “Please give some loaves of bread to my followers, for they are exhausted, and I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”

7_8_6.NKJV: And the leaders of Succoth said, “Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your army?”
7_8_6.KJV: And the princes of Succoth said, Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in thine hand, that we should give bread unto thine army?
7_8_6.ESV: And the officials of Succoth said, “Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hand, that we should give bread to your army?”
7_8_6.NASB: The leaders of Succoth said, 'Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hands, that we should give bread to your army?'
7_8_6.NLT: But the officials of Succoth replied, “Catch Zebah and Zalmunna first, and then we will feed your army.”
7_8_6.WEB: The princes of Succoth said, “Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your army?”
7_8_6.CSB17: But the princes of Succoth asked, “Are Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hands that we should give bread to your army?”
7_8_6.AMP: But the leaders of Succoth said, "Are Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hands, that we should give bread to your army?"
7_8_6.MSG: But the leaders in Succoth said, "You're on a wild goose chase; why should we help you on a fool's errand?"
7_8_6.NIV: But the officials of Succoth said, “Do you already have the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna in your possession? Why should we give bread to your troops?”
7_8_6.NRSVCE: But the officials of Succoth said, “Do you already have in your possession the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna, that we should give bread to your army?”

7_8_7.NKJV: So Gideon said, “For this cause, when the LORD has delivered Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, then I will tear your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers!”
7_8_7.KJV: And Gideon said, Therefore when the LORD hath delivered Zebah and Zalmunna into mine hand, then I will tear your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers. tear: Heb. thresh
7_8_7.ESV: So Gideon said, “Well then, when the LORD has given Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will flail your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers.”
7_8_7.NASB: Gideon said, 'All right, when the LORD has given Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, then I will thrash your bodies with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers.'
7_8_7.NLT: So Gideon said, “After the LORD gives me victory over Zebah and Zalmunna, I will return and tear your flesh with the thorns and briers from the wilderness.”
7_8_7.WEB: Gideon said, “Therefore when Yahweh has delivered Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, then I will tear your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers.”
7_8_7.CSB17: Gideon replied, “Very well, when the LORD has handed Zebah and Zalmunna over to me, I will tear your flesh with thorns and briers from the wilderness!”
7_8_7.AMP: Gideon said, "For that [response], when the LORD has handed over Zebah and Zalmunna to me, I will thrash your bodies with the thorns and briars of the wilderness."
7_8_7.MSG: Gideon said, "If you say so. But when God gives me Zebah and Zalmunna, I'll give you a thrashing, whip your bare flesh with desert thorns and thistles!"
7_8_7.NIV: Then Gideon replied, “Just for that, when the Lord has given Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will tear your flesh with desert thorns and briers.”
7_8_7.NRSVCE: Gideon replied, “Well then, when the LORD has given Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will trample your flesh on the thorns of the wilderness and on briers.”

7_8_8.NKJV: Then he went up from there to Penuel and spoke to them in the same way. And the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.KJV: And he went up thence to Penuel, and spake unto them likewise: and the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered him.
7_8_8.ESV: And from there he went up to Penuel, and spoke to them in the same way, and the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.NASB: He went up from there to Penuel and spoke similarly to them; and the men of Penuel answered him just as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.NLT: From there Gideon went up to Peniel and again asked for food, but he got the same answer.
7_8_8.WEB: He went up there to Penuel, and spoke to them in the same way; and the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.CSB17: He went from there to Penuel and asked the same thing from them. The men of Penuel answered just as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.AMP: He went from there up to Penuel and spoke similarly to them; and the men of Penuel answered him just as the men of Succoth had answered.
7_8_8.MSG: He went from there to Peniel and made the same request. The men of Peniel, like the men of Succoth, also refused.
7_8_8.NIV: From there he went up to Peniel and made the same request of them, but they answered as the men of Succoth had.
7_8_8.NRSVCE: From there he went up to Penuel, and made the same request of them; and the people of Penuel answered him as the people of Succoth had answered.

7_8_9.NKJV: So he also spoke to the men of Penuel, saying, “When I come back in peace, I will tear down this tower!”
7_8_9.KJV: And he spake also unto the men of Penuel, saying, When I come again in peace, I will break down this tower.
7_8_9.ESV: And he said to the men of Penuel, “When I come again in peace, I will break down this tower.”
7_8_9.NASB: So he spoke also to the men of Penuel, saying, 'When I return safely, I will tear down this tower.'
7_8_9.NLT: So he said to the people of Peniel, “After I return in victory, I will tear down this tower.”
7_8_9.WEB: He spoke also to the men of Penuel, saying, “When I come again in peace, I will break down this tower.”
7_8_9.CSB17: He also told the men of Penuel, “When I return safely, I will tear down this tower!”
7_8_9.AMP: So Gideon said also to the men of Penuel, "When I come again in peace, I will tear down this tower."
7_8_9.MSG: Gideon told them, "When I return safe and sound, I'll demolish this tower."
7_8_9.NIV: So he said to the men of Peniel, “When I return in triumph, I will tear down this tower.”
7_8_9.NRSVCE: So he said to the people of Penuel, “When I come back victorious, I will break down this tower.”

7_8_10.NKJV: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were at Karkor, and their armies with them, about fifteen thousand, all who were left of all the army of the people of the East; for one hundred and twenty thousand men who drew the sword had fallen.
7_8_10.KJV: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and their hosts with them, about fifteen thousand men, all that were left of all the hosts of the children of the east: for there fell an hundred and twenty thousand men that drew sword. men that: or, every one drawing a sword
7_8_10.ESV: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with their army, about 15,000 men, all who were left of all the army of the people of the East, for there had fallen 120,000 men who drew the sword.
7_8_10.NASB: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and their armies with them, about 15,000 men, all who were left of the entire army of the sons of the east; for the fallen were 120,000 swordsmen.
7_8_10.NLT: By this time Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with about 15,000 warriors — all that remained of the allied armies of the east, for 120,000 had already been killed.
7_8_10.WEB: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and their armies with them, about fifteen thousand men, all who were left of all the army of the children of the east; for there fell one hundred twenty thousand men who drew sword.
7_8_10.CSB17: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and with them was their army of about fifteen thousand men, who were all those left of the entire army of the Qedemites. Those who had been killed were one hundred twenty thousand armed men.
7_8_10.AMP: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with their armies, about fifteen thousand [fighting] men, all who were left of the entire army of the sons of the east; for a hundred and twenty thousand swordsmen had fallen.
7_8_10.MSG: Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with an army of about fifteen companies, all that was left of the fighting force of the easterners—they had lost 120 companies of soldiers.
7_8_10.NIV: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with a force of about fifteen thousand men, all that were left of the armies of the eastern peoples; a hundred and twenty thousand swordsmen had fallen.
7_8_10.NRSVCE: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with their army, about fifteen thousand men, all who were left of all the army of the people of the east; for one hundred twenty thousand men bearing arms had fallen.

7_8_11.NKJV: Then Gideon went up by the road of those who dwell in tents on the east of Nobah and Jogbehah; and he attacked the army while the camp felt secure.
7_8_11.KJV: And Gideon went up by the way of them that dwelt in tents on the east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and smote the host: for the host was secure.
7_8_11.ESV: And Gideon went up by the way of the tent dwellers east of Nobah and Jogbehah and attacked the army, for the army felt secure.
7_8_11.NASB: Gideon went up by the way of those who lived in tents on the east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and attacked the camp when the camp was unsuspecting.
7_8_11.NLT: Gideon circled around by the caravan route east of Nobah and Jogbehah, taking the Midianite army by surprise.
7_8_11.WEB: Gideon went up by the way of those who lived in tents on the east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and struck the army; for the army was secure.
7_8_11.CSB17: Gideon traveled on the caravan route east of Nobah and Jogbehah and attacked their army while the army felt secure.
7_8_11.AMP: Gideon went up by the route of those who lived in tents to the east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and he attacked their camp when the camp was unsuspecting.
7_8_11.MSG: Gideon went up the caravan trail east of Nobah and Jogbehah, found and attacked the undefended camp.
7_8_11.NIV: Gideon went up by the route of the nomads east of Nobah and Jogbehah and fell upon the unsuspecting army.
7_8_11.NRSVCE: So Gideon went up by the caravan route east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and attacked the army; for the army was off its guard.

7_8_12.NKJV: When Zebah and Zalmunna fled, he pursued them; and he took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and routed the whole army.
7_8_12.KJV: And when Zebah and Zalmunna fled, he pursued after them, and took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and discomfited all the host. discomfited: Heb. terrified
7_8_12.ESV: And Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued them and captured the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and he threw all the army into a panic.
7_8_12.NASB: When Zebah and Zalmunna fled, he pursued them and captured the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and routed the whole army.
7_8_12.NLT: Zebah and Zalmunna, the two Midianite kings, fled, but Gideon chased them down and captured all their warriors.
7_8_12.WEB: Zebah and Zalmunna fled; and he pursued them. He took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and confused all the army.
7_8_12.CSB17: Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued them. He captured these two kings of Midian and routed the entire army.
7_8_12.AMP: When Zebah and Zalmunna fled, he pursued them and captured the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and terrified the entire army.
7_8_12.MSG: Zebah and Zalmunna fled, but he chased and captured the two kings of Midian. The whole camp had panicked.
7_8_12.NIV: Zebah and Zalmunna, the two kings of Midian, fled, but he pursued them and captured them, routing their entire army.
7_8_12.NRSVCE: Zebah and Zalmunna fled; and he pursued them and took the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and threw all the army into a panic.

7_8_13.NKJV: Then Gideon the son of Joash returned from battle, from the Ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.KJV: And Gideon the son of Joash returned from battle before the sun was up,
7_8_13.ESV: Then Gideon the son of Joash returned from the battle by the ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.NASB: Then Gideon the son of Joash returned from the battle by the ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.NLT: After this, Gideon returned from the battle by way of Heres Pass.
7_8_13.WEB: Gideon the son of Joash returned from the battle from the ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.CSB17: Gideon son of Joash returned from the battle by the Ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.AMP: Then Gideon the son of Joash returned from the battle by the ascent of Heres.
7_8_13.MSG: Gideon son of Joash returned from the battle by way of the Heres Pass.
7_8_13.NIV: Gideon son of Joash then returned from the battle by the Pass of Heres.
7_8_13.NRSVCE: When Gideon son of Joash returned from the battle by the ascent of Heres,

7_8_14.NKJV: And he caught a young man of the men of Succoth and interrogated him; and he wrote down for him the leaders of Succoth and its elders, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.KJV: And caught a young man of the men of Succoth, and enquired of him: and he described unto him the princes of Succoth, and the elders thereof, even threescore and seventeen men. described: Heb. writ
7_8_14.ESV: And he captured a young man of Succoth and questioned him. And he wrote down for him the officials and elders of Succoth, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.NASB: And he captured a youth from Succoth and questioned him. Then [the youth] wrote down for him the princes of Succoth and its elders, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.NLT: There he captured a young man from Succoth and demanded that he write down the names of all the seventy-seven officials and elders in the town.
7_8_14.WEB: He caught a young man of the men of Succoth, and inquired of him: and he described for him the princes of Succoth, and its elders, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.CSB17: He captured a youth from the men of Succoth and interrogated him. The youth wrote down for him the names of the seventy-seven leaders and elders of Succoth.
7_8_14.AMP: He captured a young man of Succoth and questioned him. And the youth wrote down for him [the names of] the leaders of Succoth and its elders, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.MSG: He captured a young man from Succoth and asked some questions. The young man wrote down the names of the officials and leaders of Succoth, seventy-seven men.
7_8_14.NIV: He caught a young man of Succoth and questioned him, and the young man wrote down for him the names of the seventy-seven officials of Succoth, the elders of the town.
7_8_14.NRSVCE: he caught a young man, one of the people of Succoth, and questioned him; and he listed for him the officials and elders of Succoth, seventy-seven people.

7_8_15.NKJV: Then he came to the men of Succoth and said, “Here are Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you ridiculed me, saying, ‘Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your weary men?’ ”
7_8_15.KJV: And he came unto the men of Succoth, and said, Behold Zebah and Zalmunna, with whom ye did upbraid me, saying, Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in thine hand, that we should give bread unto thy men that are weary?
7_8_15.ESV: And he came to the men of Succoth and said, “Behold Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, ‘Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hand, that we should give bread to your men who are exhausted?’”
7_8_15.NASB: He came to the men of Succoth and said, 'Behold Zebah and Zalmunna, concerning whom you taunted me, saying, 'Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hand, that we should give bread to your men who are weary?''
7_8_15.NLT: Gideon then returned to Succoth and said to the leaders, “Here are Zebah and Zalmunna. When we were here before, you taunted me, saying, ‘Catch Zebah and Zalmunna first, and then we will feed your exhausted army.’”
7_8_15.WEB: He came to the men of Succoth, and said, “See Zebah and Zalmunna, concerning whom you taunted me, saying, ‘Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your men who are weary?’”
7_8_15.CSB17: Then he went to the men of Succoth and said, “Here are Zebah and Zalmunna. You taunted me about them, saying, ‘Are Zebah and Zalmunna now in your power that we should give bread to your exhausted men? ’”
7_8_15.AMP: He came to the men of Succoth and said, "Look here, Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, 'Are Zebah and Zalmunna now in your hand, that we should give bread to your men who are exhausted?'"
7_8_15.MSG: Then Gideon went to the men of Succoth and said, "Here are the wild geese, Zebah and Zalmunna, you said I'd never catch. You wouldn't give so much as a scrap of bread to my worn-out men; you taunted us, saying that we were on a fool's errand."
7_8_15.NIV: Then Gideon came and said to the men of Succoth, “Here are Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me by saying, ‘Do you already have the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna in your possession? Why should we give bread to your exhausted men?’”
7_8_15.NRSVCE: Then he came to the people of Succoth, and said, “Here are Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, ‘Do you already have in your possession the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna, that we should give bread to your troops who are exhausted?’”

7_8_16.NKJV: And he took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briers, and with them he taught the men of Succoth.
7_8_16.KJV: And he took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briers, and with them he taught the men of Succoth. taught: Heb. made to know
7_8_16.ESV: And he took the elders of the city, and he took thorns of the wilderness and briers and with them taught the men of Succoth a lesson.
7_8_16.NASB: He took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briers, and he disciplined the men of Succoth with them.
7_8_16.NLT: Then Gideon took the elders of the town and taught them a lesson, punishing them with thorns and briers from the wilderness.
7_8_16.WEB: He took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briers, and with them he taught the men of Succoth.
7_8_16.CSB17: So he took the elders of the city, and he took some thorns and briers from the wilderness, and he disciplined the men of Succoth with them.
7_8_16.AMP: He took the elders of the city, and thorns of the wilderness and briars, and with them he punished the men of Succoth.
7_8_16.MSG: Then he took the seventy-seven leaders of Succoth and thrashed them with desert thorns and thistles.
7_8_16.NIV: He took the elders of the town and taught the men of Succoth a lesson by punishing them with desert thorns and briers.
7_8_16.NRSVCE: So he took the elders of the city and he took thorns of the wilderness and briers and with them he trampled the people of Succoth.

7_8_17.NKJV: Then he tore down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.KJV: And he beat down the tower of Penuel, and slew the men of the city.
7_8_17.ESV: And he broke down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.NASB: He tore down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.NLT: He also tore down the tower of Peniel and killed all the men in the town.
7_8_17.WEB: He broke down the tower of Penuel, and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.CSB17: He also tore down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.AMP: He tore down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.MSG: And he demolished the tower of Peniel and killed the men of the city.
7_8_17.NIV: He also pulled down the tower of Peniel and killed the men of the town.
7_8_17.NRSVCE: He also broke down the tower of Penuel, and killed the men of the city.

7_8_18.NKJV: And he said to Zebah and Zalmunna, “What kind of men were they whom you killed at Tabor?” So they answered, “As you are, so were they; each one resembled the son of a king.”
7_8_18.KJV: Then said he unto Zebah and Zalmunna, What manner of men were they whom ye slew at Tabor? And they answered, As thou art, so were they; each one resembled the children of a king. resembled: Heb. according to the form, etc
7_8_18.ESV: Then he said to Zebah and Zalmunna, “Where are the men whom you killed at Tabor?” They answered, “As you are, so were they. Every one of them resembled the son of a king.”
7_8_18.NASB: Then he said to Zebah and Zalmunna, 'What kind of men [were] they whom you killed at Tabor?' And they said, 'They were like you, each one resembling the son of a king.'
7_8_18.NLT: Then Gideon asked Zebah and Zalmunna, “The men you killed at Tabor — what were they like?” “Like you,” they replied. “They all had the look of a king’s son.”
7_8_18.WEB: Then he said to Zebah and Zalmunna, “What kind of men were they whom you killed at Tabor?” They answered, “They were like you. Each one resembled the children of a king.”
7_8_18.CSB17: He asked Zebah and Zalmunna, “What kind of men did you kill at Tabor?” “They were like you,” they said. “Each resembled the son of a king.”
7_8_18.AMP: Then Gideon said to Zebah and Zalmunna, "What kind of men were they whom you killed at Tabor?" And they replied, "They were like you, each one of them resembled the son of a king."
7_8_18.MSG: He then addressed Zebah and Zalmunna: "Tell me about the men you killed at Tabor.""They were men much like you," they said, "each one like a king's son."
7_8_18.NIV: Then he asked Zebah and Zalmunna, “What kind of men did you kill at Tabor?”“Men like you,” they answered, “each one with the bearing of a prince.”
7_8_18.NRSVCE: Then he said to Zebah and Zalmunna, “What about the men whom you killed at Tabor?” They answered, “As you are, so were they, every one of them; they resembled the sons of a king.”

7_8_19.NKJV: Then he said, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother. As the LORD lives, if you had let them live, I would not kill you.”
7_8_19.KJV: And he said, They were my brethren, even the sons of my mother: as the LORD liveth, if ye had saved them alive, I would not slay you.
7_8_19.ESV: And he said, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother. As the LORD lives, if you had saved them alive, I would not kill you.”
7_8_19.NASB: He said, 'They [were] my brothers, the sons of my mother. [As] the LORD lives, if only you had let them live, I would not kill you.'
7_8_19.NLT: “They were my brothers, the sons of my own mother!” Gideon exclaimed. “As surely as the LORD lives, I wouldn’t kill you if you hadn’t killed them.”
7_8_19.WEB: He said, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother. As Yahweh lives, if you had saved them alive, I would not kill you.”
7_8_19.CSB17: So he said, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother! As the LORD lives, if you had let them live, I would not kill you.”
7_8_19.AMP: He said, "They were my brothers, the sons of my mother. As the LORD lives, if only you had let them live, I would not kill you."
7_8_19.MSG: Gideon said, "They were my brothers, my mother's sons. As God lives, if you had let them live, I would let you live."
7_8_19.NIV: Gideon replied, “Those were my brothers, the sons of my own mother. As surely as the Lord lives, if you had spared their lives, I would not kill you.”
7_8_19.NRSVCE: And he replied, “They were my brothers, the sons of my mother; as the LORD lives, if you had saved them alive, I would not kill you.”

7_8_20.NKJV: And he said to Jether his firstborn, “Rise, kill them!” But the youth would not draw his sword; for he was afraid, because he was still a youth.
7_8_20.KJV: And he said unto Jether his firstborn, Up, and slay them. But the youth drew not his sword: for he feared, because he was yet a youth.
7_8_20.ESV: So he said to Jether his firstborn, “Rise and kill them!” But the young man did not draw his sword, for he was afraid, because he was still a young man.
7_8_20.NASB: So he said to Jether his firstborn, 'Rise, kill them.' But the youth did not draw his sword, for he was afraid, because he was still a youth.
7_8_20.NLT: Turning to Jether, his oldest son, he said, “Kill them!” But Jether did not draw his sword, for he was only a boy and was afraid.
7_8_20.WEB: He said to Jether his firstborn, “Get up, and kill them!” But the youth didn’t draw his sword; for he was afraid, because he was yet a youth.
7_8_20.CSB17: Then he said to Jether, his firstborn, “Get up and kill them.” The youth did not draw his sword, for he was afraid because he was still a youth.
7_8_20.AMP: So [to humiliate them] Gideon said to Jether his firstborn, "Stand up, and kill them!" But the youth did not draw his sword, because he was afraid, for he was still [just] a boy.
7_8_20.MSG: Then he spoke to Jether, his firstborn: "Get up and kill them." But he couldn't do it, couldn't draw his sword. He was afraid—he was still just a boy.
7_8_20.NIV: Turning to Jether, his oldest son, he said, “Kill them!” But Jether did not draw his sword, because he was only a boy and was afraid.
7_8_20.NRSVCE: So he said to Jether his firstborn, “Go kill them!” But the boy did not draw his sword, for he was afraid, because he was still a boy.

7_8_21.NKJV: So Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Rise yourself, and kill us; for as a man is, so is his strength.” So Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescent ornaments that were on their camels’ necks.
7_8_21.KJV: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, Rise thou, and fall upon us: for as the man is, so is his strength. And Gideon arose, and slew Zebah and Zalmunna, and took away the ornaments that were on their camels' necks. ornaments: or, ornaments like the moon
7_8_21.ESV: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Rise yourself and fall upon us, for as the man is, so is his strength.” And Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and he took the crescent ornaments that were on the necks of their camels.
7_8_21.NASB: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, 'Rise up yourself, and fall on us; for as the man, so is his strength.' So Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescent ornaments which were on their camels' necks.
7_8_21.NLT: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said to Gideon, “Be a man! Kill us yourself!” So Gideon killed them both and took the royal ornaments from the necks of their camels.
7_8_21.WEB: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Rise and fall on us; for as the man is, so is his strength.” Gideon arose, and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescents that were on their camels’ necks.
7_8_21.CSB17: Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Get up and strike us down yourself, for a man is judged by his strength.” So Gideon got up, killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescent ornaments that were on the necks of their camels.
7_8_21.AMP: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, "Rise up yourself and strike us; for as the man is, so is his strength." So Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescent amulets that were on their camels' necks.
7_8_21.MSG: Zebah and Zalmunna said, "Do it yourself—if you're man enough!" And Gideon did it. He stepped up and killed Zebah and Zalmunna. Then he took the crescents that hung on the necks of their camels.
7_8_21.NIV: Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Come, do it yourself. ‘As is the man, so is his strength.’” So Gideon stepped forward and killed them, and took the ornaments off their camels’ necks.
7_8_21.NRSVCE: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, “You come and kill us; for as the man is, so is his strength.” So Gideon proceeded to kill Zebah and Zalmunna; and he took the crescents that were on the necks of their camels.

7_8_22.NKJV: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, “Rule over us, both you and your son, and your grandson also; for you have delivered us from the hand of Midian.”
7_8_22.KJV: Then the men of Israel said unto Gideon, Rule thou over us, both thou, and thy son, and thy son's son also: for thou hast delivered us from the hand of Midian.
7_8_22.ESV: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, “Rule over us, you and your son and your grandson also, for you have saved us from the hand of Midian.”
7_8_22.NASB: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, 'Rule over us, both you and your son, also your son's son, for you have delivered us from the hand of Midian.'
7_8_22.NLT: Then the Israelites said to Gideon, “Be our ruler! You and your son and your grandson will be our rulers, for you have rescued us from Midian.”
7_8_22.WEB: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, “Rule over us, both you, and your son, and your son’s son also; for you have saved us out of the hand of Midian.”
7_8_22.CSB17: Then the Israelites said to Gideon, “Rule over us, you as well as your sons and your grandsons, for you delivered us from the power of Midian.”
7_8_22.AMP: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, "Rule [as king] over us, both you and your son, also your son's son, for you have rescued us from the hand of Midian."
7_8_22.MSG: The Israelites said, "Rule over us, you and your son and your grandson. You have saved us from Midian's tyranny."
7_8_22.NIV: Gideon’s EphodThe Israelites said to Gideon, “Rule over us—you, your son and your grandson—because you have saved us out of the hand of Midian.”
7_8_22.NRSVCE: Then the Israelites said to Gideon, “Rule over us, you and your son and your grandson also; for you have delivered us out of the hand of Midian.”

7_8_23.NKJV: But Gideon said to them, “I will not rule over you, nor shall my son rule over you; the LORD shall rule over you.”
7_8_23.KJV: And Gideon said unto them, I will not rule over you, neither shall my son rule over you: the LORD shall rule over you.
7_8_23.ESV: Gideon said to them, “I will not rule over you, and my son will not rule over you; the LORD will rule over you.”
7_8_23.NASB: But Gideon said to them, 'I will not rule over you, nor shall my son rule over you; the LORD shall rule over you.'
7_8_23.NLT: But Gideon replied, “I will not rule over you, nor will my son. The LORD will rule over you!
7_8_23.WEB: Gideon said to them, “I will not rule over you, neither shall my son rule over you. Yahweh shall rule over you.”
7_8_23.CSB17: But Gideon said to them, “I will not rule over you, and my son will not rule over you; the LORD will rule over you.”
7_8_23.AMP: But Gideon said to them, "I will not rule over you, and my son will not rule over you; the LORD shall rule over you."
7_8_23.MSG: Gideon said, "I most certainly will not rule over you, nor will my son. God will reign over you."
7_8_23.NIV: But Gideon told them, “I will not rule over you, nor will my son rule over you. The Lord will rule over you.”
7_8_23.NRSVCE: Gideon said to them, “I will not rule over you, and my son will not rule over you; the LORD will rule over you.”

7_8_24.NKJV: Then Gideon said to them, “I would like to make a request of you, that each of you would give me the earrings from his plunder.” For they had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.
7_8_24.KJV: And Gideon said unto them, I would desire a request of you, that ye would give me every man the earrings of his prey. (For they had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)
7_8_24.ESV: And Gideon said to them, “Let me make a request of you: every one of you give me the earrings from his spoil.” (For they had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)
7_8_24.NASB: Yet Gideon said to them, 'I would request of you, that each of you give me an earring from his spoil.' (For they had gold earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)
7_8_24.NLT: However, I do have one request — that each of you give me an earring from the plunder you collected from your fallen enemies.” (The enemies, being Ishmaelites, all wore gold earrings.)
7_8_24.WEB: Gideon said to them, “I do have a request, that you would each give me the earrings of his plunder.” (For they had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)
7_8_24.CSB17: Then he said to them, “Let me make a request of you: Everyone give me an earring from his plunder.” Now the enemy had gold earrings because they were Ishmaelites.
7_8_24.AMP: And Gideon said to them, "I would make a request of you, that each one of you give me an earring from his spoil." For the Midianites had gold earrings, because they were Ishmaelites [who customarily wore them].
7_8_24.MSG: Then Gideon said, "But I do have one request. Give me, each of you, an earring that you took as plunder." Ishmaelites wore gold earrings, and the men all had their pockets full of them.
7_8_24.NIV: And he said, “I do have one request, that each of you give me an earring from your share of the plunder.” (It was the custom of the Ishmaelites to wear gold earrings.)
7_8_24.NRSVCE: Then Gideon said to them, “Let me make a request of you; each of you give me an earring he has taken as booty.” (For the enemy had golden earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)

7_8_25.NKJV: So they answered, “We will gladly give them.” And they spread out a garment, and each man threw into it the earrings from his plunder.
7_8_25.KJV: And they answered, We will willingly give them. And they spread a garment, and did cast therein every man the earrings of his prey.
7_8_25.ESV: And they answered, “We will willingly give them.” And they spread a cloak, and every man threw in it the earrings of his spoil.
7_8_25.NASB: They said, 'We will surely give [them].' So they spread out a garment, and every one of them threw an earring there from his spoil.
7_8_25.NLT: “Gladly!” they replied. They spread out a cloak, and each one threw in a gold earring he had gathered from the plunder.
7_8_25.WEB: They answered, “We will willingly give them.” They spread a garment, and every man threw the earrings of his plunder into it.
7_8_25.CSB17: They said, “We agree to give them.” So they spread out a cloak, and everyone threw an earring from his plunder on it.
7_8_25.AMP: They answered, "We will certainly give them to you." And they spread out a garment, and every one of them threw an earring there from his spoil.
7_8_25.MSG: They said, "Of course. They're yours!"They spread out a blanket and each man threw his plundered earrings on it.
7_8_25.NIV: They answered, “We’ll be glad to give them.” So they spread out a garment, and each man threw a ring from his plunder onto it.
7_8_25.NRSVCE: “We will willingly give them,” they answered. So they spread a garment, and each threw into it an earring he had taken as booty.

7_8_26.NKJV: Now the weight of the gold earrings that he requested was one thousand seven hundred shekels of gold, besides the crescent ornaments, pendants, and purple robes which were on the kings of Midian, and besides the chains that were around their camels’ necks.
7_8_26.KJV: And the weight of the golden earrings that he requested was a thousand and seven hundred shekels of gold; beside ornaments, and collars, and purple raiment that was on the kings of Midian, and beside the chains that were about their camels' necks. collars: or, sweet jewels
7_8_26.ESV: And the weight of the golden earrings that he requested was 1,700 shekels of gold, besides the crescent ornaments and the pendants and the purple garments worn by the kings of Midian, and besides the collars that were around the necks of their camels.
7_8_26.NASB: The weight of the gold earrings that he requested was 1,700 [shekels] of gold, besides the crescent ornaments and the pendants and the purple robes which [were] on the kings of Midian, and besides the neck bands that [were] on their camels' necks.
7_8_26.NLT: The weight of the gold earrings was forty-three pounds, not including the royal ornaments and pendants, the purple clothing worn by the kings of Midian, or the chains around the necks of their camels.
7_8_26.WEB: The weight of the golden earrings that he requested was one thousand and seven hundred shekels of gold, besides the crescents, and the pendants, and the purple clothing that was on the kings of Midian, and besides the chains that were about their camels’ necks.
7_8_26.CSB17: The weight of the gold earrings he requested was forty-three pounds of gold, in addition to the crescent ornaments and ear pendants, the purple garments on the kings of Midian, and the chains on the necks of their camels.
7_8_26.AMP: And the weight of the golden earrings that he requested was seventeen hundred shekels of gold, apart from the crescent amulets and pendants and the purple garments which were worn by the kings of Midian, and apart from the chains that were on their camels' necks.
7_8_26.MSG: The gold earrings that Gideon had asked for weighed about forty-three pounds—and that didn't include the crescents and pendants, the purple robes worn by the Midianite kings, and the ornaments hung around the necks of their camels.
7_8_26.NIV: The weight of the gold rings he asked for came to seventeen hundred shekels, not counting the ornaments, the pendants and the purple garments worn by the kings of Midian or the chains that were on their camels’ necks.
7_8_26.NRSVCE: The weight of the golden earrings that he requested was one thousand seven hundred shekels of gold (apart from the crescents and the pendants and the purple garments worn by the kings of Midian, and the collars that were on the necks of their camels).

7_8_27.NKJV: Then Gideon made it into an ephod and set it up in his city, Ophrah. And all Israel played the harlot with it there. It became a snare to Gideon and to his house.
7_8_27.KJV: And Gideon made an ephod thereof, and put it in his city, even in Ophrah: and all Israel went thither a whoring after it: which thing became a snare unto Gideon, and to his house.
7_8_27.ESV: And Gideon made an ephod of it and put it in his city, in Ophrah. And all Israel whored after it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and to his family.
7_8_27.NASB: Gideon made it into an ephod, and placed it in his city, Ophrah, and all Israel played the harlot with it there, so that it became a snare to Gideon and his household.
7_8_27.NLT: Gideon made a sacred ephod from the gold and put it in Ophrah, his hometown. But soon all the Israelites prostituted themselves by worshiping it, and it became a trap for Gideon and his family.
7_8_27.WEB: Gideon made an ephod out of it, and put it in Ophrah, his city. Then all Israel played the prostitute with it there; and it became a snare to Gideon, and to his house.
7_8_27.CSB17: Gideon made an ephod from all this and put it in Ophrah, his hometown. Then all Israel prostituted themselves by worshiping it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and his household.
7_8_27.AMP: Gideon made [all the golden earrings into] an ephod [a sacred, high priest's garment], and put it in his city of Ophrah, and all Israel worshiped it as an idol there, and it became a trap for Gideon and his household.
7_8_27.MSG: Gideon made the gold into a sacred ephod and put it on display in his hometown, Ophrah. All Israel prostituted itself there. Gideon and his family, too, were seduced by it.
7_8_27.NIV: Gideon made the gold into an ephod, which he placed in Ophrah, his town. All Israel prostituted themselves by worshiping it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and his family.
7_8_27.NRSVCE: Gideon made an ephod of it and put it in his town, in Ophrah; and all Israel prostituted themselves to it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and to his family.

7_8_28.NKJV: Thus Midian was subdued before the children of Israel, so that they lifted their heads no more. And the country was quiet for forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.KJV: Thus was Midian subdued before the children of Israel, so that they lifted up their heads no more. And the country was in quietness forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.ESV: So Midian was subdued before the people of Israel, and they raised their heads no more. And the land had rest forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.NASB: So Midian was subdued before the sons of Israel, and they did not lift up their heads anymore. And the land was undisturbed for forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.NLT: That is the story of how the people of Israel defeated Midian, which never recovered. Throughout the rest of Gideon’s lifetime — about forty years — there was peace in the land.
7_8_28.WEB: So Midian was subdued before the children of Israel, and they lifted up their heads no more. The land had rest forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.CSB17: So Midian was subdued before the Israelites, and they were no longer a threat. The land had peace for forty years during the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.AMP: So Midian was subdued and humbled before the sons of Israel, and they no longer lifted up their heads [in pride]. And the land was at rest for forty years in the days of Gideon.
7_8_28.MSG: Midian's tyranny was broken by the Israelites; nothing more was heard from them. The land was quiet for forty years in Gideon's time.
7_8_28.NIV: Gideon’s DeathThus Midian was subdued before the Israelites and did not raise its head again. During Gideon’s lifetime, the land enjoyed peace forty years.
7_8_28.NRSVCE: So Midian was subdued before the Israelites, and they lifted up their heads no more. So the land had rest forty years in the days of Gideon.

7_8_33.NKJV: So it was, as soon as Gideon was dead, that the children of Israel again played the harlot with the Baals, and made Baal-Berith their god.
7_8_33.KJV: And it came to pass, as soon as Gideon was dead, that the children of Israel turned again, and went a whoring after Baalim, and made Baalberith their god.
7_8_33.ESV: As soon as Gideon died, the people of Israel turned again and whored after the Baals and made Baal-berith their god.
7_8_33.NASB: Then it came about, as soon as Gideon was dead, that the sons of Israel again played the harlot with the Baals, and made Baal-berith their god.
7_8_33.NLT: As soon as Gideon died, the Israelites prostituted themselves by worshiping the images of Baal, making Baal-berith their god.
7_8_33.WEB: As soon as Gideon was dead, the children of Israel turned again, and played the prostitute following the Baals, and made Baal Berith their god.
7_8_33.CSB17: When Gideon died, the Israelites turned and prostituted themselves by worshiping the Baals and made Baal-berith their god.
7_8_33.AMP: Then it came about, as soon as Gideon was dead, that the Israelites again played the prostitute with the Baals, and made Baal-berith their god.
7_8_33.MSG: Gideon was hardly cool in the tomb when the People of Israel had gotten off track and were prostituting themselves to Baal—they made Baal-of-the-Covenant their god.
7_8_33.NIV: No sooner had Gideon died than the Israelites again prostituted themselves to the Baals. They set up Baal-Berith as their god
7_8_33.NRSVCE: As soon as Gideon died, the Israelites relapsed and prostituted themselves with the Baals, making Baal-berith their god.

7_8_34.NLT: They forgot the LORD their God, who had rescued them from all their enemies surrounding them.

7_9_2.NKJV: “Please speak in the hearing of all the men of Shechem: ‘Which is better for you, that all seventy of the sons of Jerubbaal reign over you, or that one reign over you?’ Remember that I am your own flesh and bone.”
7_9_2.KJV: Speak, I pray you, in the ears of all the men of Shechem, Whether is better for you, either that all the sons of Jerubbaal, which are threescore and ten persons, reign over you, or that one reign over you? remember also that I am your bone and your flesh. Whether: Heb. What is good? whether, etc
7_9_2.ESV: “Say in the ears of all the leaders of Shechem, ‘Which is better for you, that all seventy of the sons of Jerubbaal rule over you, or that one rule over you?’ Remember also that I am your bone and your flesh.”
7_9_2.NASB: 'Speak, now, in the hearing of all the leaders of Shechem, 'Which is better for you, that seventy men, all the sons of Jerubbaal, rule over you, or that one man rule over you?' Also, remember that I am your bone and your flesh.'
7_9_2.NLT: “Ask the leading citizens of Shechem whether they want to be ruled by all seventy of Gideon’s sons or by one man. And remember that I am your own flesh and blood!”
7_9_2.WEB: “Please speak in the ears of all the men of Shechem, ‘Is it better for you that all the sons of Jerubbaal, who are seventy persons, rule over you, or that one rule over you?’ Remember also that I am your bone and your flesh.”
7_9_2.CSB17: “Please speak in the hearing of all the citizens of Shechem, ‘Is it better for you that seventy men, all the sons of Jerubbaal, rule over you or that one man rule over you? ’ Remember that I am your own flesh and blood.”
7_9_2.AMP: "Speak now in the hearing of all the leaders of Shechem, 'Which is better for you, that seventy men, all of the sons of Jerubbaal rule over you, or that one man rule over you?' Also, remember that I am your own bone and flesh."
7_9_2.MSG: "Ask all the leading men of Shechem, 'What do you think is best, that seventy men rule you—all those sons of Jerub-Baal—or that one man rule? You'll remember that I am your own flesh and blood.' "

7_9_3.NKJV: And his mother’s brothers spoke all these words concerning him in the hearing of all the men of Shechem; and their heart was inclined to follow Abimelech, for they said, “He is our brother.”

7_9_24.NKJV: that the crime done to the seventy sons of Jerubbaal might be settled and their blood be laid on Abimelech their brother, who killed them, and on the men of Shechem, who aided him in the killing of his brothers.
7_9_24.KJV: That the cruelty done to the threescore and ten sons of Jerubbaal might come, and their blood be laid upon Abimelech their brother, which slew them; and upon the men of Shechem, which aided him in the killing of his brethren. aided: Heb. strengthened his hands to kill
7_9_24.ESV: that the violence done to the seventy sons of Jerubbaal might come, and their blood be laid on Abimelech their brother, who killed them, and on the men of Shechem, who strengthened his hands to kill his brothers.
7_9_24.NASB: so that the violence done to the seventy sons of Jerubbaal might come, and their blood might be laid on Abimelech their brother, who killed them, and on the men of Shechem, who strengthened his hands to kill his brothers.
7_9_24.NLT: God was punishing Abimelech for murdering Gideon’s seventy sons, and the citizens of Shechem for supporting him in this treachery of murdering his brothers.

7_9_27.NKJV: So they went out into the fields, and gathered grapes from their vineyards and trod them, and made merry. And they went into the house of their god, and ate and drank, and cursed Abimelech.

7_9_28.NKJV: Then Gaal the son of Ebed said, “Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal, and is not Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem; but why should we serve him?
7_9_28.KJV: And Gaal the son of Ebed said, Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? is not he the son of Jerubbaal? and Zebul his officer? serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem: for why should we serve him?
7_9_28.ESV: And Gaal the son of Ebed said, “Who is Abimelech, and who are we of Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal, and is not Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem; but why should we serve him?
7_9_28.NASB: Then Gaal the son of Ebed said, 'Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal, and [is] Zebul [not] his lieutenant? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem; but why should we serve him?
7_9_28.NLT: “Who is Abimelech?” Gaal shouted. “He’s not a true son of Shechem, so why should we be his servants? He’s merely the son of Gideon, and this Zebul is merely his deputy. Serve the true sons of Hamor, the founder of Shechem. Why should we serve Abimelech?
7_9_28.WEB: Gaal the son of Ebed said, “Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Isn’t he the son of Jerubbaal? Isn’t Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem, but why should we serve him?
7_9_28.CSB17: Gaal son of Ebed said, “Who is Abimelech and who is Shechem that we should serve him? Isn’t he the son of Jerubbaal, and isn’t Zebul his officer? You are to serve the men of Hamor, the father of Shechem. Why should we serve Abimelech?
7_9_28.AMP: Gaal the son of Ebed said, "Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not [merely] the son of Jerubbaal and is Zebul not his lieutenant? Serve the men of Hamor the father (founder) of Shechem. Why then should we serve Abimelech?
7_9_28.MSG: Gaal son of Ebed said, "Who is this Abimelech? And who are we Shechemites to take orders from him? Isn't he the son of Jerub-Baal, and isn't this his henchman Zebul? We belong to the race of Hamor and bear the noble name of Shechem. Why should we be toadies of Abimelech?
7_9_28.NIV: Then Gaal son of Ebed said, “Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should be subject to him? Isn’t he Jerub-Baal’s son, and isn’t Zebul his deputy? Serve the men of Hamor, Shechem’s father! Why should we serve Abimelech?

7_10_6.NKJV: Then the children of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, and served the Baals and the Ashtoreths, the gods of Syria, the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the people of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines; and they forsook the LORD and did not serve Him.

7_10_6.AMP: Then the Israelites again did what was evil in the sight of the LORD; they served the Baals, the Ashtaroth (female deities), the gods of Aram (Syria), the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the Ammonites, and the gods of the Philistines. They abandoned the LORD and did not serve Him.

And because the Israelites forsook the Lord and no longer served him,

7_11_1.MSG: Jephthah the Gileadite was one tough warrior. He was the son of a whore, but Gilead was his father.
7_11_1.NIV: Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty warrior. His father was Gilead; his mother was a prostitute.
7_11_1.NRSVCE: Now Jephthah the Gileadite, the son of a prostitute, was a mighty warrior. Gilead was the father of Jephthah.

7_11_2.NKJV: Gilead’s wife bore sons; and when his wife’s sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out, and said to him, “You shall have no inheritance in our father’s house, for you are the son of another woman.”
7_11_2.KJV: And Gilead's wife bare him sons; and his wife's sons grew up, and they thrust out Jephthah, and said unto him, Thou shalt not inherit in our father's house; for thou art the son of a strange woman.
7_11_2.ESV: And Gilead's wife also bore him sons. And when his wife's sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out and said to him, “You shall not have an inheritance in our father's house, for you are the son of another woman.”
7_11_2.NASB: Gilead's wife bore him sons; and when his wife's sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out and said to him, 'You shall not have an inheritance in our father's house, for you are the son of another woman.'
7_11_2.NLT: Gilead’s wife also had several sons, and when these half brothers grew up, they chased Jephthah off the land. “You will not get any of our father’s inheritance,” they said, “for you are the son of a prostitute.”
7_11_2.WEB: Gilead’s wife bore him sons. When his wife’s sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out, and said to him, “You will not inherit in our father’s house, for you are the son of another woman.”
7_11_2.CSB17: Gilead’s wife bore him sons, and when they grew up, they drove Jephthah out and said to him, “You will have no inheritance in our father’s family, because you are the son of another woman.”
7_11_2.AMP: Gilead's wife bore him sons, and when his wife's sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out and said to him, "You shall not have an inheritance in our father's house, because you are the son of another woman."
7_11_2.MSG: Meanwhile Gilead's legal wife had given him other sons, and when they grew up, his wife's sons threw Jephthah out. They told him: "You're not getting any of our family inheritance—you're the son of another woman."
7_11_2.NIV: Gilead’s wife also bore him sons, and when they were grown up, they drove Jephthah away. “You are not going to get any inheritance in our family,” they said, “because you are the son of another woman.”
7_11_2.NRSVCE: Gilead’s wife also bore him sons; and when his wife’s sons grew up, they drove Jephthah away, saying to him, “You shall not inherit anything in our father’s house; for you are the son of another woman.”

7_11_3.NKJV: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and dwelt in the land of Tob; and worthless men banded together with Jephthah and went out raiding with him.
7_11_3.KJV: Then Jephthah fled from his brethren, and dwelt in the land of Tob: and there were gathered vain men to Jephthah, and went out with him. from: Heb. from the face
7_11_3.ESV: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob, and worthless fellows collected around Jephthah and went out with him.
7_11_3.NASB: So Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob; and worthless fellows gathered themselves about Jephthah, and they went out with him.
7_11_3.NLT: So Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Soon he had a band of worthless rebels following him.
7_11_3.WEB: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers, and lived in the land of Tob. Outlaws joined up with Jephthah, and they went out with him.
7_11_3.CSB17: So Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Then some worthless men joined Jephthah and went on raids with him.
7_11_3.AMP: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob; and worthless and unprincipled men gathered around Jephthah, and went out [on raids] with him.
7_11_3.MSG: So Jephthah fled from his brothers and went to live in the land of Tob. Some riff-raff joined him and went around with him.
7_11_3.NIV: So Jephthah fled from his brothers and settled in the land of Tob, where a group of adventurers gathered around him and followed him.
7_11_3.NRSVCE: Then Jephthah fled from his brothers and lived in the land of Tob. Outlaws collected around Jephthah and went raiding with him.

7_11_4.NKJV: It came to pass after a time that the people of Ammon made war against Israel.

7_11_4.ESV: After a time the Ammonites made war against Israel.
7_11_4.NASB: It came about after a while that the sons of Ammon fought against Israel.
7_11_4.NLT: At about this time, the Ammonites began their war against Israel.
7_11_4.WEB: After a while, the children of Ammon made war against Israel.
7_11_4.CSB17: Some time later, the Ammonites fought against Israel.
7_11_4.AMP: Now it happened after a while that the Ammonites fought against Israel.
7_11_4.MSG: Some time passed. And then the Ammonites started fighting Israel.
7_11_4.NIV: Some time later, when the Ammonites made war on Israel,
7_11_4.NRSVCE: After a time the Ammonites made war against Israel.

7_11_5.NKJV: And so it was, when the people of Ammon made war against Israel, that the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob.
7_11_5.KJV: And it was so, that when the children of Ammon made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to fetch Jephthah out of the land of Tob:
7_11_5.ESV: And when the Ammonites made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to bring Jephthah from the land of Tob.
7_11_5.NASB: When the sons of Ammon fought against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob;
7_11_5.NLT: When the Ammonites attacked, the elders of Gilead sent for Jephthah in the land of Tob.
7_11_5.WEB: When the children of Ammon made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah out of the land of Tob.
7_11_5.CSB17: When the Ammonites made war with Israel, the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob.
7_11_5.AMP: When the Ammonites fought against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob;
7_11_5.MSG: With the Ammonites at war with them, the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob.
7_11_5.NIV: the elders of Gilead went to get Jephthah from the land of Tob.
7_11_5.NRSVCE: And when the Ammonites made war against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to bring Jephthah from the land of Tob.

7_11_6.NKJV: Then they said to Jephthah, “Come and be our commander, that we may fight against the people of Ammon.”
7_11_6.KJV: And they said unto Jephthah, Come, and be our captain, that we may fight with the children of Ammon.
7_11_6.ESV: And they said to Jephthah, “Come and be our leader, that we may fight against the Ammonites.”
7_11_6.NASB: and they said to Jephthah, 'Come and be our chief that we may fight against the sons of Ammon.'
7_11_6.NLT: The elders said, “Come and be our commander! Help us fight the Ammonites!”
7_11_6.WEB: They said to Jephthah, “Come and be our chief, that we may fight with the children of Ammon.”
7_11_6.CSB17: They said to him, “Come, be our commander, and let’s fight the Ammonites.”
7_11_6.AMP: and they said to Jephthah, "Come and be our leader, so that we may fight against the Ammonites."
7_11_6.MSG: They said to Jephthah: "Come. Be our general and we'll fight the Ammonites."
7_11_6.NIV: “Come,” they said, “be our commander, so we can fight the Ammonites.”
7_11_6.NRSVCE: They said to Jephthah, “Come and be our commander, so that we may fight with the Ammonites.”

7_11_7.NKJV: So Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “Did you not hate me, and expel me from my father’s house? Why have you come to me now when you are in distress?”
7_11_7.KJV: And Jephthah said unto the elders of Gilead, Did not ye hate me, and expel me out of my father's house? and why are ye come unto me now when ye are in distress?
7_11_7.ESV: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “Did you not hate me and drive me out of my father's house? Why have you come to me now when you are in distress?”
7_11_7.NASB: Then Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, 'Did you not hate me and drive me from my father's house? So why have you come to me now when you are in trouble?'
7_11_7.NLT: But Jephthah said to them, “Aren’t you the ones who hated me and drove me from my father’s house? Why do you come to me now when you’re in trouble?”
7_11_7.WEB: Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “Didn’t you hate me, and drive me out of my father’s house? Why have you come to me now when you are in distress?”
7_11_7.CSB17: Jephthah replied to the elders of Gilead, “Didn’t you hate me and drive me out of my father’s family? Why then have you come to me now when you’re in trouble?”
7_11_7.AMP: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, "Did you not hate me and drive me from the house of my father? Why have you come to me now when you are in trouble?"
7_11_7.MSG: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead: "But you hate me. You kicked me out of my family home. So why are you coming to me now? Because you are in trouble. Right?"
7_11_7.NIV: Jephthah said to them, “Didn’t you hate me and drive me from my father’s house? Why do you come to me now, when you’re in trouble?”
7_11_7.NRSVCE: But Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “Are you not the very ones who rejected me and drove me out of my father’s house? So why do you come to me now when you are in trouble?”

7_11_8.NKJV: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “That is why we have turned again to you now, that you may go with us and fight against the people of Ammon, and be our head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
7_11_8.KJV: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah, Therefore we turn again to thee now, that thou mayest go with us, and fight against the children of Ammon, and be our head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.
7_11_8.ESV: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “That is why we have turned to you now, that you may go with us and fight against the Ammonites and be our head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
7_11_8.NASB: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, 'For this reason we have now returned to you, that you may go with us and fight with the sons of Ammon and become head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.'
7_11_8.NLT: “Because we need you,” the elders replied. “If you lead us in battle against the Ammonites, we will make you ruler over all the people of Gilead.”
7_11_8.WEB: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “Therefore we have turned again to you now, that you may go with us, and fight with the children of Ammon. You will be our head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
7_11_8.CSB17: They answered Jephthah, “That’s true. But now we turn to you. Come with us, fight the Ammonites, and you will become leader of all the inhabitants of Gilead.”
7_11_8.AMP: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, "This is why we have turned to you now: that you may go with us and fight the Ammonites and become head over all the inhabitants of Gilead."
7_11_8.MSG: The elders of Gilead replied, "That's it exactly. We've come to you to get you to go with us and fight the Ammonites. You'll be the head of all of us, all the Gileadites."
7_11_8.NIV: The elders of Gilead said to him, “Nevertheless, we are turning to you now; come with us to fight the Ammonites, and you will be our head over all who live in Gilead.”
7_11_8.NRSVCE: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “Nevertheless, we have now turned back to you, so that you may go with us and fight with the Ammonites, and become head over us, over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”

7_11_9.NKJV: So Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “If you take me back home to fight against the people of Ammon, and the LORD delivers them to me, shall I be your head?”
7_11_9.KJV: And Jephthah said unto the elders of Gilead, If ye bring me home again to fight against the children of Ammon, and the LORD deliver them before me, shall I be your head?
7_11_9.ESV: Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “If you bring me home again to fight against the Ammonites, and the LORD gives them over to me, I will be your head.”
7_11_9.NASB: So Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, 'If you take me back to fight against the sons of Ammon and the LORD gives them up to me, will I become your head?'
7_11_9.NLT: Jephthah said to the elders, “Let me get this straight. If I come with you and if the LORD gives me victory over the Ammonites, will you really make me ruler over all the people?”
7_11_9.WEB: Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “If you bring me home again to fight with the children of Ammon, and Yahweh delivers them before me, will I be your head?”
7_11_9.CSB17: So Jephthah said to them, “If you are bringing me back to fight the Ammonites and the LORD gives them to me, I will be your leader.”
7_11_9.AMP: So Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, "If you take me back [home] to fight against the Ammonites and the LORD gives them over to me, will I [really] become your head?"
7_11_9.MSG: Jephthah addressed the elders of Gilead, "So if you bring me back home to fight the Ammonites and God gives them to me, I'll be your head—is that right?"
7_11_9.NIV: Jephthah answered, “Suppose you take me back to fight the Ammonites and the Lord gives them to me—will I really be your head?”
7_11_9.NRSVCE: Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, “If you bring me home again to fight with the Ammonites, and the LORD gives them over to me, I will be your head.”

7_11_10.NKJV: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The LORD will be a witness between us, if we do not do according to your words.”
7_11_10.KJV: And the elders of Gilead said unto Jephthah, The LORD be witness between us, if we do not so according to thy words. witness: Heb. the hearer between us
7_11_10.ESV: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The LORD will be witness between us, if we do not do as you say.”
7_11_10.NASB: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, 'The LORD is witness between us; surely we will do as you have said.'
7_11_10.NLT: “The LORD is our witness,” the elders replied. “We promise to do whatever you say.”
7_11_10.WEB: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “Yahweh will be witness between us. Surely we will do what you say.”
7_11_10.CSB17: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The LORD is our witness if we don’t do as you say.”
7_11_10.AMP: The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, "The LORD is the witness between us; be assured that we will do as you have said."
7_11_10.MSG: They said, "God is witness between us; whatever you say, we'll do."
7_11_10.NIV: The elders of Gilead replied, “The Lord is our witness; we will certainly do as you say.”
7_11_10.NRSVCE: And the elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, “The LORD will be witness between us; we will surely do as you say.”

7_11_11.NKJV: Then Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and commander over them; and Jephthah spoke all his words before the LORD in Mizpah.

7_11_12.ESV: Then Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites and said, “What do you have against me, that you have come to me to fight against my land?”
7_11_12.NASB: Now Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the sons of Ammon, saying, 'What is between you and me, that you have come to me to fight against my land?'

7_11_13.NKJV: And the king of the people of Ammon answered the messengers of Jephthah, “Because Israel took away my land when they came up out of Egypt, from the Arnon as far as the Jabbok, and to the Jordan. Now therefore, restore those lands peaceably.”
7_11_13.KJV: And the king of the children of Ammon answered unto the messengers of Jephthah, Because Israel took away my land, when they came up out of Egypt, from Arnon even unto Jabbok, and unto Jordan: now therefore restore those lands again peaceably.
7_11_13.ESV: And the king of the Ammonites answered the messengers of Jephthah, “Because Israel on coming up from Egypt took away my land, from the Arnon to the Jabbok and to the Jordan; now therefore restore it peaceably.”
7_11_13.NASB: The king of the sons of Ammon said to the messengers of Jephthah, 'Because Israel took away my land when they came up from Egypt, from the Arnon as far as the Jabbok and the Jordan; therefore, return them peaceably now.'
7_11_13.NLT: The king of Ammon answered Jephthah’s messengers, “When the Israelites came out of Egypt, they stole my land from the Arnon River to the Jabbok River and all the way to the Jordan. Now then, give back the land peaceably.”
7_11_13.WEB: The king of the children of Ammon answered to the messengers of Jephthah, “Because Israel took away my land, when he came up out of Egypt, from the Arnon even to the Jabbok, and to the Jordan. Now therefore restore that territory again peaceably.”

7_11_13.MSG: The king of the Ammonites told Jephthah's messengers: "Because Israel took my land when they came up out of Egypt—from the Arnon all the way to the Jabbok and to the Jordan. Give it back peaceably and I'll go."
7_11_13.NIV: The king of the Ammonites answered Jephthah’s messengers, “When Israel came up out of Egypt, they took away my land from the Arnon to the Jabbok, all the way to the Jordan. Now give it back peaceably.”
7_11_13.NRSVCE: The king of the Ammonites answered the messengers of Jephthah, “Because Israel, on coming from Egypt, took away my land from the Arnon to the Jabbok and to the Jordan; now therefore restore it peaceably.”

7_11_17.NKJV: Then Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, “Please let me pass through your land.” But the king of Edom would not heed. And in like manner they sent to the king of Moab, but he would not consent. So Israel remained in Kadesh.
7_11_17.KJV: Then Israel sent messengers unto the king of Edom, saying, Let me, I pray thee, pass through thy land: but the king of Edom would not hearken thereto. And in like manner they sent unto the king of Moab: but he would not consent: and Israel abode in Kadesh.
7_11_17.ESV: Israel then sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, ‘Please let us pass through your land,’ but the king of Edom would not listen. And they sent also to the king of Moab, but he would not consent. So Israel remained at Kadesh.
7_11_17.NASB: then Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, 'Please let us pass through your land,' but the king of Edom would not listen. And they also sent to the king of Moab, but he would not consent. So Israel remained at Kadesh.

7_11_17.WEB: then Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, ‘Please let me pass through your land;’ but the king of Edom didn’t listen. In the same way, he sent to the king of Moab; but he would not: and Israel stayed in Kadesh.
7_11_17.CSB17: Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, ‘Please let us travel through your land,’ but the king of Edom would not listen. They also sent messengers to the king of Moab, but he refused. So Israel stayed in Kadesh.

7_11_17.MSG: There Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom saying, 'Let us pass through your land, please.' But the king of Edom wouldn't let them. Israel also requested permission from the king of Moab, but he wouldn't let them cross either. They were stopped in their tracks at Kadesh.
7_11_17.NIV: Then Israel sent messengers to the king of Edom, saying, ‘Give us permission to go through your country,’ but the king of Edom would not listen. They sent also to the king of Moab, and he refused. So Israel stayed at Kadesh.

7_11_18.NKJV: And they went along through the wilderness and bypassed the land of Edom and the land of Moab, came to the east side of the land of Moab, and encamped on the other side of the Arnon. But they did not enter the border of Moab, for the Arnon was the border of Moab.
7_11_18.KJV: Then they went along through the wilderness, and compassed the land of Edom, and the land of Moab, and came by the east side of the land of Moab, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, but came not within the border of Moab: for Arnon was the border of Moab.
7_11_18.ESV: “Then they journeyed through the wilderness and went around the land of Edom and the land of Moab and arrived on the east side of the land of Moab and camped on the other side of the Arnon. But they did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon was the boundary of Moab.
7_11_18.NASB: 'Then they went through the wilderness and around the land of Edom and the land of Moab, and came to the east side of the land of Moab, and they camped beyond the Arnon; but they did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon [was] the border of Moab.
7_11_18.NLT: “Finally, they went around Edom and Moab through the wilderness. They traveled along Moab’s eastern border and camped on the other side of the Arnon River. But they never once crossed the Arnon River into Moab, for the Arnon was the border of Moab.
7_11_18.WEB: Then they went through the wilderness, and went around the land of Edom, and the land of Moab, and came by the east side of the land of Moab, and they encamped on the other side of the Arnon; but they didn’t come within the border of Moab, for the Arnon was the border of Moab.
7_11_18.CSB17: “Then they traveled through the wilderness and around the lands of Edom and Moab. They came to the east side of the land of Moab and camped on the other side of the Arnon but did not enter into the territory of Moab, for the Arnon was the boundary of Moab.
7_11_18.AMP: Then they went through the wilderness and went around the land of Edom and the land of Moab, and came to the east side of the land of Moab, and they camped on the other side of the [river] Arnon; but they did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon was the [northern] boundary of Moab.
7_11_18.MSG: So they traveled across the desert and circled around the lands of Edom and Moab. They came out east of the land of Moab and set camp on the other side of the Arnon—they didn't set foot in Moabite territory, for Arnon was the Moabite border.
7_11_18.NIV: “Next they traveled through the desert, skirted the lands of Edom and Moab, passed along the eastern side of the country of Moab, and camped on the other side of the Arnon. They did not enter the territory of Moab, for the Arnon was its border.

7_11_23.NKJV: ‘And now the LORD God of Israel has dispossessed the Amorites from before His people Israel; should you then possess it?
7_11_23.KJV: So now the LORD God of Israel hath dispossessed the Amorites from before his people Israel, and shouldest thou possess it?

7_11_23.NASB: 'Since now the LORD, the God of Israel, drove out the Amorites from before His people Israel, are you then to possess it?
7_11_23.NLT: “So you see, it was the LORD, the God of Israel, who took away the land from the Amorites and gave it to Israel. Why, then, should we give it back to you?
7_11_23.WEB: So now Yahweh, the God of Israel, has dispossessed the Amorites from before his people Israel, and should you possess them?
7_11_23.CSB17: “The LORD God of Israel has now driven out the Amorites before his people Israel, and will you now force us out?
7_11_23.AMP: And now the LORD God of Israel has dispossessed and driven out the Amorites from before His people Israel, so [why] should you possess it?
7_11_23.MSG: It was God, the God of Israel, who pushed out the Amorites in favor of Israel; so who do you think you are to try to take it over?
7_11_23.NIV: “Now since the Lord, the God of Israel, has driven the Amorites out before his people Israel, what right have you to take it over?
7_11_23.NRSVCE: So now the LORD, the God of Israel, has conquered the Amorites for the benefit of his people Israel. Do you intend to take their place?

7_11_24.NKJV: Will you not possess whatever Chemosh your god gives you to possess? So whatever the LORD our God takes possession of before us, we will possess.

7_11_24.NASB: 'Do you not possess what Chemosh your god gives you to possess? So whatever the LORD our God has driven out before us, we will possess it.
7_11_24.NLT: You keep whatever your god Chemosh gives you, and we will keep whatever the LORD our God gives us.
7_11_24.WEB: Won’t you possess that which Chemosh your god gives you to possess? So whoever Yahweh our God has dispossessed from before us, them will we possess.

7_11_24.AMP: Do you not possess what Chemosh your god gives you to possess? And everything that the LORD our God dispossessed before us, we will possess.

7_11_24.NRSVCE: Should you not possess what your god Chemosh gives you to possess? And should we not be the ones to possess everything that the LORD our God has conquered for our benefit?
